question: Does Alabama tax military retirement pay?
summary: Alabama Military Retired Pay Income Tax Exemption: Military retired pay is exempt from Alabama income tax.
detailMd:
Alabama Military Retired Pay Income Tax Exemption: Military retired pay is exempt from Alabama income tax.

Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) does not withhold taxes for state or local income tax, but it is reported annually on IRS Form 1099-R. All TSP distributions are subject to state taxes and early distributions may incur higher taxes.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Alabama?
summary: Alabama Homestead Tax Exemption for Residents over age 65, Blind or Totally Disabled: Alabama offers a homestead exemption for all state, county and city property taxes for residents who meet the following requirements: * Alabama resident * Over age 65 whose adjusted gross income is less than $12,000, or * Has a total and permanent disability, regardless of age or income A homestead is defined as the single-family owner-occupied home and the land it is on, not exceeding 160 acres.
detailMd:
Alabama Homestead Tax Exemption for Residents over age 65, Blind or Totally Disabled: Alabama offers a homestead exemption for all state, county and city property taxes for residents who meet the following requirements:

*   Alabama resident
*   Over age 65 whose adjusted gross income is less than $12,000, or
*   Has a total and permanent disability, regardless of age or income

A homestead is defined as the single-family owner-occupied home and the land it is on, not exceeding 160 acres. The property owner must own and occupy it as their primary residence on the first day of the tax year they are applying for an exemption. For more information, please contact the Alabama Department of Revenue, County Offices where the property is located for more information and to apply.

question: Does Alaska tax military retirement pay?
summary: Alaska Income Taxes:There are no individual income taxes in Alaska.
detailMd: Alaska Income Taxes:There are no individual income taxes in Alaska. Distributions received from the Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) are not taxed.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Alaska?
summary: Alaska Property Tax Exemption for Disabled Veterans: Real property owned and occupied as a primary residence by a Veteran who has a 50% or more service-connected disability rating from the VA is exempt from property taxes on the first $150,000 of assessed value.
detailMd: Alaska Property Tax Exemption for Disabled Veterans: Real property owned and occupied as a primary residence by a Veteran who has a 50% or more service-connected disability rating from the VA is exempt from property taxes on the first $150,000 of assessed value. This exemption transfers to a spouse if the Veteran passes away and the spouse is at least 60 years of age. For more information, please contact the local Alaska Tax Office.
disabilityThreshold: 50% or more service-connected disability rating from the VA is exempt from property taxes on the first $150,000 of assessed value

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Arizona?
summary: Arizona Property Tax Exemptions for Disabled Veterans and Surviving Spouses: The property of a resident disabled Veteran who ha s a service- or non-service-connected total and permanent disability rating from the VA are eligible for a complete property tax exemption.
detailMd:
Arizona Property Tax Exemptions for Disabled Veterans and Surviving Spouses: The property  of a resident disabled Veteran who ha s a service- or non-service-connected total and permanent disability rating from the VA are eligible for a complete property tax exemption.

Veterans who do not have a 100% total and permanent disability rating are eligible for an exemption up to $4,748 of the assessed value of their property. To determine the amount of exemption, multiply $4,748 by the Veteran’s VA disability percentage. T he t otal assessed value of property must not exceed $3 6,454.

Applicants mus t also meet the following income limitations:

*   Total Income – no minor children, $ 3 9,865
*   Total Income – minor children or children with disability, $ 4 7,826

*see the affidavit below for monetary amounts

question: Does California tax military retirement pay?
summary: California Taxes on Military Retired Pay: Beginning in tax year 2025 (taxes filed after 1 January 2026, and before January 1, 2030), California has authorized a tax exclusion of $20,000 of military retired pay.
detailMd:
California Taxes on Military Retired Pay: Beginning in tax year 2025 (taxes filed after 1 January 2026, and before January 1, 2030), California has authorized a tax exclusion of $20,000 of military retired pay. This exclusion is limited to taxpayers with an income that is up to $125,000 for those filing single or head-of-household, and up to $250,000 for those filing jointly.

question: Does Colorado tax military retirement pay?

question: Does Connecticut tax military retirement pay?
summary: Connecticut State Tax Exemption for Military Retired Pay:Retired service members do not have to pay Connecticut income taxes on military retired pay if their military retired pay is taxed on their federal income tax return.
detailMd:
Connecticut State Tax Exemption for Military Retired Pay:Retired service members do not have to pay Connecticut income taxes on military retired pay if their military retired pay is taxed on their federal income tax return.

Former spouses of retired service members who receive part of the service member’s military retired pay under a final decree of divorce, dissolution, annulment, legal separation, or a court ordered property settlement do not qualify for the 100% military retired pay tax exemption.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Connecticut?
summary: Connecticut Veteran Property Tax Exemptions:Recent legislation signed by the governor mandates that municipalities must offer a a complete property tax exemption for Veterans who have a total and permanent service-connected disability rating from the VA.
detailMd: Connecticut Veteran Property Tax Exemptions:Recent legislation signed by the governor mandates that municipalities must offer a a complete property tax exemption for Veterans who have a total and permanent service-connected disability rating from the VA. Eligible Veterans who do not own a home will be authorized a complete property tax exemption on one motor vehicle. This exemption will take effect 1 October 2024 and will not be reflected until their 2026 tax bill. Veterans may not simultaneously receive other authorized exemptions if they receive the total and permanent exemption.

question: Does Georgia tax military retirement pay?
summary: Georgia Tax Exemption for Military Retired Pay: Retired service members are eligible for the following state tax exemptions on military retired pay: * Under 62 years old - up to $17,500 * Under 62 years old and have at least $17,500 of earned income – an additional $17,500 * 62 to 64 years old - up to $35,000 * 65 years old or older - up to $65,000 Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) does not withhold taxes for state or local income tax, but it is reported annually on IRS Form 1099-R.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Illinois?
summary: Illinois Disabled Veterans' Standard Homestead Exemption (DVSHE): DVSHE provides a reduction in a property's equalized assessed value (EAV) of a property owned by a Veteran who has a service-connected disability.
detailMd:
Illinois Disabled Veterans' Standard Homestead Exemption (DVSHE): DVSHE provides a reduction in a property's equalized assessed value (EAV) of a property owned by a Veteran who has a service-connected disability. The property's total EAV must be less than $250,000 to be eligible for this exemption. The reduction is based on the percentage of the Veteran’s disability:

*   $2,500 exemption for a service-connected disability rating of at least 30% but less than 50%
*   $5,000 for a service-connected disability rating of at least 50% but less than 70%
*   Total exemption for a service-connected disability rating of 70% or more

This exemption is also available to the unremarried surviving spouse of a Veteran who received an exemption prior to their death. If a surviving spouse moves to a new home, they are eligible to receive an exemption equal to the amount they received for their previous home. If the Veteran died from a service-connected cause and was not receiving a property tax exemption, their unremarried surviving spouse who is receiving Disability Dependency and Indemnity Compensation (DIC) is eligible to apply.

Additionally, the unremarried surviving spouse of a service member killed in the line of duty is exempt from all property taxes on their primary residence.

For tax years on or after 2024, if the Veteran served in the U. S. Armed Forces during World War II, then the property is exempt from taxation regardless of the Veteran’s disability level.

Applicants should contact local County Assessor's Office for more information and to apply.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Indiana?
summary: Vehicle Excise Tax Deduction for Disabled Veterans:Veterans or their spouse who are eligible for any of the deductions above, but the assessed value of their property is less than the deduction can apply the overage toward the excise tax on up to two vehicles.
detailMd:
Vehicle Excise Tax Deduction for Disabled Veterans:Veterans or their spouse who are eligible for any of the deductions above, but the assessed value of their property is less than the deduction can apply the overage toward the excise tax on up to two vehicles. Veterans and their eligible spouse can receive $2 credit to be applied toward the excise tax for every $100 of remaining property tax deduction up to $70. An individual cannot claim a vehicle credit and a property tax credit at the same time.

Veterans and their eligible spouses must request a [Indiana Department of Veterans Affairs (IDVA), Certificate of Eligibility for Disabled Veteran Property Tax Deduction

After receiving their certificate of eligibility applicants can apply by submitting their completed certificate and an [Application for Tax Deduction for Disabled Veterans and Surviving Spouses of Certain Veterans to their local county auditor.

Iowa Taxes on U. S. Department of Veterans Affairs Military Disability Retirement Pay: Military disability retirement pay received as a pension, annuity or similar allowance for personal injury or sickness resulting from active service in the U. S. Armed Forces should not be included in taxable income. Some of the payments which are considered disability benefits include:

*   Disability compensation and pension payments for disabilities paid to Veterans or their families
*   Grants for homes designed for wheelchair living
*   Grants for motor vehicles for Veterans who lost their sight or the use of limbs, or
*   Benefits under a dependent-care assistance program

See [IRS Publication 525

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Iowa?
summary: Iowa Disabled Veteran's Homestead Tax Credit:Iowa offers a 100% property tax credit for homesteads owned by eligible Veterans and the surviving spouse of an eligible Veteran.
detailMd:
Iowa Disabled Veteran's Homestead Tax Credit:Iowa offers a 100% property tax credit for homesteads owned by eligible Veterans and the surviving spouse of an eligible Veteran. The Veteran must own and occupy the property as a homestead on July 1 of each year, declare residency in Iowa for income tax purposes, and occupy the property for at least six months each year. Service members serving in the U. S. Armed Forces and nursing home residents who do not occupy the home are also eligible.

This exemption is limited to a maximum size of 1/2 acre when the home and outbuildings are located within a city limit. For properties located outside city limits the maximum size is 40 acres including the house and any outbuildings.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Kansas?
summary: Kansas Homestead Refund for Disabled Veterans and Surviving Spouses: The Kansas Department of Revenue offers a Homestead Refund for Disabled Veterans, their Surviving Spouse and the Surviving Spouse of a Service member killed in the line of duty.
detailMd:
Kansas Homestead Refund for Disabled Veterans and Surviving Spouses: The Kansas Department of Revenue offers a Homestead Refund for Disabled Veterans, their Surviving Spouse and the Surviving Spouse of a Service member killed in the line of duty.

The Homestead Refund is a rebate program for the property taxes paid by homeowners. The refund is based on a portion of the property tax paid on a Kansas resident’s home up to $700. The appraised value of the home cannot exceed $350,000 and total household income cannot exceed $56,450.

question: Does Kentucky tax military retirement pay?
summary: Kentucky Military Retired Pay Income Taxes: The military retired pay of Service members who retired before January 1, 1998, is completely exempt From Kentucky income taxes.
detailMd:
Kentucky Military Retired Pay Income Taxes: The military retired pay of Service members who retired before January 1, 1998, is completely exempt From Kentucky income taxes. Service members who retired from the U. S. Armed Forces after December 31, 1997, may exclude up to $31,110 of their military retired pay from Kentucky income taxes.

Retired Service members who have creditable service prior to January 1, 1998, but retired after December 31, 1997, may be able to exclude more than $31,110. To determine the additional percentage of their military retired pay that may be excluded for service prior to January 1, 1998 retired Service members must complete the [Kentucky Pension Income Exclusion, Schedule P. 

Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) does not withhold taxes for state or local income tax, but it is reported annually on IRS Form 1099-R. All TSP distributions are subject to state taxes and early distributions may incur higher taxes.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Kentucky?
summary: Kentucky Property Tax Exemption for Disabled Veterans:Kentucky homeowners who are least 65 years old or who are totally disabled are eligible for a homestead exemption from Kentucky property taxes.
detailMd:
Kentucky Property Tax Exemption for Disabled Veterans:Kentucky homeowners who are least 65 years old or who are totally disabled are eligible for a homestead exemption from Kentucky property taxes. The adjustment for the 2025-2026 assessment years is $49,100. This amount is deducted from the assessed value of the Veteran’s home and property taxes are computed based on the remaining assessment. The amount of the homestead exemption is recalculated every two years to adjust for inflation. 

To apply for a homestead exemption an applicant must:

*   Complete the [Kentucky Department of Revenue Application for Exemption Under The Homestead/Disability Amendment - Form 62 A350%20updated%20 Fillable.pdf " Kentucky Department of Revenue Application for Exemption Under The Homestead/Disability Amendment - Form 62 A350 ​ ")
*   Gather any supporting documentation (listed on the application)
*   Apply at their local Property Value Administrator (PVA)

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Louisiana?
summary: Disabled Veteran Exemption: The amount of the disabled Veterans property tax exemption is based on the Veterans disability percentage: * Service-connected disability rating of 100%, individual unemployability, or totally disabled is exempt from all property taxes * Service-connected disability rating of 70-99% is eligible for an additional exemption of $4,500 of the assessed value * Service-connected disability rating of 50-69% is eligible for an additional exemption of $2,500 of the assessed value The surviving spouse of an eligible deceased disabled Veteran is also eligible for this exemption if they remain the owner and live on the property.
detailMd:
Disabled Veteran Exemption: The amount of the disabled Veterans property tax exemption is based on the Veterans disability percentage:

*   Service-connected disability rating of 100%, individual unemployability, or totally disabled is exempt from all property taxes
*   Service-connected disability rating of 70-99% is eligible for an additional exemption of $4,500 of the assessed value
*   Service-connected disability rating of 50-69% is eligible for an additional exemption of $2,500 of the assessed value

The surviving spouse of an eligible deceased disabled Veteran is also eligible for this exemption if they remain the owner and live on the property.
disabilityThreshold: 100%, individual unemployability, or totally disabled is exempt from all property taxes

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Maryland?
summary: Maryland Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption:Maryland offers a property tax exemption to Veterans who have a service-connected 100% total and permanent or unemployable disability rating from the VA.
detailMd:
Maryland Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption:Maryland offers a property tax exemption to Veterans who have a service-connected 100% total and permanent or unemployable disability rating from the VA. Service members who have received a service-connected 100% total and permanent disability rating from their branch of service are also eligible. This is a complete exemption from real property taxes on their house and surrounding yard. This exemption is available to unremarried surviving spouses of eligible Veterans and to surviving spouses of service members killed in the line of duty.

For more information applicants should contact their local Maryland Assessment Office.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Michigan?
summary: Who is eligible for the Michigan Disabled Veteran Homestead Property Tax Credit? To be eligible for the exemption the Veteran must be a Michigan resident, received an honorable discharge from the U.S.
detailMd:
Who is eligible for the Michigan Disabled Veteran Homestead Property Tax Credit? To be eligible for the exemption the Veteran must be a Michigan resident, received an honorable discharge from the U. S. Armed Forces and meet one of the following requirements:

*   Total and permanent service-connected disability rating from the VA
*   Received financial assistance from the VA to purchase, construct, or modify an existing home to accommodate their disability
*   Rated by the VA as individually unemployable

An unremarried, resident surviving spouse of a disabled Veteran is also eligible for this exemption.

To apply applicants must submit a completed [Affidavit for Disabled Veterans Exemption

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Minnesota?
summary: Minnesota Market Value Exclusion on Homestead Property of Disabled Veterans, Primary Family Caregivers and Surviving Spouses: Minnesota offers a market value exclusion program for Veterans, surviving spouses, or a primary family caregiver of a Veteran.
detailMd:
Minnesota Market Value Exclusion on Homestead Property of Disabled Veterans, Primary Family Caregivers and Surviving Spouses: Minnesota offers a market value exclusion program for Veterans, surviving spouses, or a primary family caregiver of a Veteran. This program reduces the market value of homesteads for tax purposes, which may reduce property tax. This program provides two different levels of market value exclusions:

*   $150,000 exclusion for: 
    *   Veterans with at least a 70% service-connected disa bility rating but less than a 100% rating
    *   Primary family caregiver of a Veteran with at least a 70% service-connected disability rating but less than a 100% rating 

*   $300,000 exclusion for: 
    *   Veterans with a 100% total and permanent, service-connected disability rating
    *   Surviving spouse receiving Dependency Indemnity Compensation (DIC)
    *   Surviving spouse of a Veteran who had a 100% total and permanent, service-connected disability rating
    *   Primary family caregiver of a Veteran with a 100% total and permanent, service-connected disability rating
disabilityThreshold: 70% service-connected disability rating but less than a 100% rating

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Mississippi?
summary: Mississippi Homestead Property Tax Exemptions for Veterans and Surviving Spouses:Mississippi offers property tax exemption to Disabled Veterans, Veterans who are over 90 years old, surviving spouses of eligible Veterans, as well as the surviving spouses of service members killed while serving in an active or reserve component of the U.S.
detailMd:
Mississippi Homestead Property Tax Exemptions for Veterans and Surviving Spouses:Mississippi offers property tax exemption to Disabled Veterans, Veterans who are over 90 years old, surviving spouses of eligible Veterans, as well as the surviving spouses of service members killed while serving in an active or reserve component of the U. S. Armed Forces. Eligible individuals are exempt from all ad valorem taxes on their assessed homestead value. Who is eligible for the Mississippi Homestead Property Tax Exemptions?The following individuals are eligible for a property tax exemption:

*   Honorably discharged Veterans who have a service-connected total (100%) disability rating from the VA and their surviving spouse
*   Honorably discharged Veterans who are 90 years of age or older and their surviving spouse
*   Surviving spouses of a service member who died while serving on active duty in the U. S. Armed Forces
*   Surviving spouses of a service member who died while serving in a reserve component the U. S. Armed Forces in an active duty for training status

For more information and to apply, please contact the local tax assessor office in the county where the property is located.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Missouri?
summary: Missouri Property Tax Credit for 100% Disabled Veterans:The Missouri Property Tax Credit gives credit to eligible resident Veterans who have a service-connected 100% disability rating from the VA for a portion of the real estate taxes or rent paid for the year.
detailMd:
Missouri Property Tax Credit for 100% Disabled Veterans:The Missouri Property Tax Credit gives credit to eligible resident Veterans who have a service-connected 100% disability rating from the VA for a portion of the real estate taxes or rent paid for the year. The credit is for up to a maximum of $750 for renters and up to $1,100 for homeowners who are within the income limits listed below:

*   Renters and part year owners - $27,200 or less for single filers; $29,200 or less for married filing combined
*   Owned and occupied home for entire year - $30,000 or less for single filers, $34,000 or less for married filing combined

Renters who rent from a tax free facility are not eligible. For more information and to apply, please contact the Missouri County Assessor’s Office where the property is located.

question: Does Montana tax military retirement pay?
summary: Montana Military Retired Pay Income Taxes: Montana resident retired service members who are working in the state may be eligible for an exemption of part of their military retired pay.
detailMd:
Montana Military Retired Pay Income Taxes: Montana resident retired service members who are working in the state may be eligible for an exemption of part of their military retired pay.

This exemption is available for working retired service members who become Montana residents after June 30, 2023, or were a Montana resident before they began receiving military retired pay. The exemption is equal to the lesser of half of the taxpayer’s military retirement income or the amount of Montana source wage income. This exemption is for five consecutive years after meeting eligibility requirements and is scheduled to expire in tax year 2033.

detailMd:
Montana Disabled Veteran Assistance Program (MDV), Property Tax Reduction:  MDV reduces  the property tax rate on the homes of disabled Veterans or their unremarried surviving spouse.

For homes on agricultural or forest land, the benefit applies to the home and the one-acre homes site.

Depending on the marital status and income of the homeowner, the tax rate is reduced by 100%, 80%, 70% or 50% of the normal tax rate. The income ranges are updated each year for inflation.

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in Oklahoma?
summary: Oklahoma 100% Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemption: Oklahoma offers a 100% property tax exemption for eligible disabled Veterans and their surviving spouse.
detailMd:
Oklahoma 100% Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemption:  Oklahoma offers a 100% property tax exemption for eligible disabled Veterans and their surviving spouse. This exemption is for the full fair cash value of the homestead.  Who is eligible for the Oklahoma 100% Disabled Veteran Property Tax Exemption?  To be eligible the applicants must meet the following requirements:

* Oklahoma resident

* Applicant is the owner of the homestead

* Veteran who received an honorable discharge from active service in the U. S. Armed Forces or from the Oklahoma National Guard

* Veteran has a permanent service-connected disability and are receiving compensation from the VA at the 100% rate, or

* Surviving spouse of an eligible Veteran

Applicants must complete the [Oklahoma Tax Commission Form 998, Application for 100% Disabled Veterans Real Property Tax Exemption and provide documentation from the VA specifically for this exemption to their local county assessor. Applicants can request documentation from the VA using the [Oklahoma Department of Veterans Affairs, Online State Benefit Request Form. [Oklahoma County Assessor Directory

Oklahoma Social Security Benefits Tax Subtraction: Oklahoma does not tax Social Security benefits that are included in a resident’s federal adjusted gross income.

question: Does Oregon tax military retirement pay?
summary: Oregon Military Retired Pay Income Taxes: Retired service members can subtract military retired pay earned for service prior to October 1, 1991, from their Oregon income taxes.
detailMd:
Oregon Military Retired Pay Income Taxes: Retired service members can subtract military retired pay earned for service prior to October 1, 1991, from their Oregon income taxes. Any military retired pay earned on or after 1 October 1991 may not be subtracted.

If service included months before and after October1, 1, 1991, retired service members can subtract a percentage of their pension income. To determine the percentage, divide the months of service before October 1, 1991, by the total months of service. Percentages must be rounded to three places (example: 0.4576 = 45.8%).

Once a service member retires, withdrawals from their Thrift Savings Plan (TSP) account are eligible for this subtraction based on dates of service. Withdrawals prior to retirement are not eligible for subtraction. If the taxpayer moves money from a TSP to another type of account, the account is no longer a federal pension, and future withdrawals are not eligible for subtraction.

For more information, contact the [Oregon Department of Revenue.

detailMd:
Texas Homestead Tax Exemption for 100% Disabled or Unemployable Veterans: Property tax in Texas is a locally assessed and locally administered tax by local Appraisal Districts and Tax Assessors or Collectors.. The Comptroller's publication [Texas Property Tax Exemptions ")

Veterans who receive compensation from the VA for a service-connected 100% total and permanent disability rating or of individual unemployability are eligible for 100% property tax exemption on their homestead.

An unremarried surviving spouse can continue to receive this exemption if they continue to live on the homestead after the Veteran dies. A surviving spouse can receive this exemption on a subsequent homestead if they do not remarry. The amount of the exemption on the subsequent homestead will be the same dollar amount of the exemption on the first homestead.

A Veteran who qualifies for more than one property exemption is only eligible for one exemption but may choose the greatest exemption they qualify for. For more information, please contact the County Appraisal District, Tax Assessor or Collector. [Texas Property Tax Code 11.131

question: Does Vermont tax military retirement pay?
summary: Vermont Income Taxes on Military Retired Pay: The Governor of Vermont recently signed a new law that creates a tiered exemption for military retired pay, effective beginning in the 2025 tax year (taxes filed after 1 January 2026).
detailMd:
Vermont Income Taxes on Military Retired Pay: The Governor of Vermont recently signed a new law that creates a tiered exemption for military retired pay, effective beginning in the 2025 tax year (taxes filed after 1 January 2026).

*   Taxpayers with a federal adjusted gross income (AGI) of $125,000 or less will be exempt from state taxes on all military retired pay
*   Taxpayers with a federal AGI between $125,000 and $175,000, a partial exemption will be applied; calculated by $175,000 minus AGI, divided by $50,000, and then multiplied by the total amount of military retired pay
*   Taxpayers with a federal AGI of more than $175,000 will not be eligible for this exemption

question: What disabled veteran property tax relief is available in West Virginia?
summary: West Virginia Disabled Veteran Homestead Property Tax Credit: Honorably discharged disabled Veterans who have a 90% or greater combined disability rating from the VA are eligible for an income tax credit for the amount they paid in property taxes for their homestead.
detailMd:
West Virginia Disabled Veteran Homestead Property Tax Credit: Honorably discharged disabled Veterans who have a 90% or greater combined disability rating from the VA are eligible for an income tax credit for the amount they paid in property taxes for their homestead. Homesteads are defined as a single family residential house, including a mobile, manufactured, or modular home, and the land surrounding the homestead. Veterans must have used or occupied the homestead for at least six months to be eligible. Surviving spouses of eligible Veterans may claim this credit.

Veterans are still required to pay their property taxes (by October 1 and April 1) and will receive the credit when they file their personal income taxes. If a Veteran owns several properties, the credit will only apply to the property where they are primarily living.

For more information, please contact the county tax assessor where the property is located, call a Taxpayer Services Representative at 304-558-3333, 800-982-8297, or email [taxhelp@wv.gov](mailto:taxhelp@wv.gov).  [West Virginia County Assessor Directory [West Virginia Code 11-13 MM-4 [West Virginia Department of Revenue, Disabled Veteran Real Property Tax Credit Fact Sheet

summary: Wyoming disabled veteran property tax rules are published in the official state benefits guide.
detailMd: Summary of Wyoming Military and Veterans Benefits: Wyoming offers special benefits for service members, Veterans and their families including state Veteran Service Officer assistance, Veterans Property Tax Exemption, Military Assistance Trust Fund, Veterans’ preference in employment, employment and training services, unemployment benefits, special vehicle license plates, education benefits, hunting and fishing license benefits and state park privileges. Eligibility for some benefits may depend on residency, military component, and Veteran disability status.
